articles.headline: National Urban Transformation Mission (NUTM) Launched: A Blueprint for Sustainable Indian Cities
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister unveils the ambitious NUTM, aiming for holistic urban development across 100 cities over the next decade.
• Focus areas include smart infrastructure, sustainable mobility, green spaces, waste management, and digital governance.
• The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships (PPPs) and citizen participation for effective implementation.
• Aims to address challenges of rapid urbanization, improve liveability, and boost urban economic growth.
• Integrated with existing initiatives like Smart Cities Mission and Swachh Bharat Abhiyan, with a significant allocation for climate-resilient infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: NUTM launched by Prime Minister on Jan 22, 2026.
Targets 100 cities over 10 years.
Key pillars: infrastructure, mobility, green spaces, waste management, digital governance.
Emphasizes PPP model.
Integrates with Smart Cities Mission and Swachh Bharat Abhiyan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of NUTM in addressing India's urban challenges, linking it to SDGs.
Analyze the role of PPPs and citizen participation in achieving sustainable urban development.
Evaluate the potential impact of NUTM on economic growth, environmental sustainability, and social equity in urban areas.
Examine the challenges in implementing such a large-scale mission, including funding, coordination, and capacity building.
articles.imageDescription: Conceptual image of a futuristic, green Indian city with smart infrastructure.
articles.trivia: India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030, making sustainable urban planning critical.

articles.headline: 10 Soldiers Killed, 10 Injured as Indian Army Casspir Falls into Gorge in Doda, J&K
articles.explainer:
• A tragic incident occurred in Doda district, Jammu and Kashmir, when an Indian Army Casspir vehicle veered off road and plunged into a deep gorge.
• The accident resulted in the immediate death of 10 soldiers and left 10 others seriously injured, raising concerns about road safety in challenging terrains.
• The Casspir, an armored personnel carrier, was reportedly part of a routine patrol in the mountainous region.
• Rescue operations were immediately launched by the Army and local authorities, with injured personnel airlifted to military hospitals.
• Investigations are underway to determine the exact cause of the accident, including potential mechanical failure or hazardous road conditions.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Internal security
prelimsPoints: Incident occurred in Doda, Jammu & Kashmir.
Vehicle involved: Indian Army Casspir.
Casualties: 10 killed, 10 injured.
Context: Routine patrol in mountainous terrain.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by Indian security forces operating in difficult terrains like J&K, including logistical and infrastructure issues.
Analyze the implications of such incidents on troop morale and operational effectiveness.
Suggest measures to enhance road safety and vehicle maintenance for military convoys in sensitive areas.
Examine the broader internal security challenges in Jammu and Kashmir and the role of the Indian Army.
articles.imageDescription: Mountainous terrain of Jammu and Kashmir, with a military vehicle on a winding road.
articles.trivia: The Casspir is a mine-protected ambush-protected (MRAP) vehicle originally developed in South Africa.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% in Q4 Monetary Policy Review; Focus on Inflation Control
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision aligns with market expectations, prioritizing inflation control amidst global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor highlighted persistent food inflation and geopolitical tensions as key risks to price stability.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Projections for Q4 FY26 GDP growth and inflation trajectory were also released, indicating a cautious optimism about economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI Repo Rate maintained at 6.5% on Jan 22, 2026.
Decision by Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Focus on inflation control, 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
Key risks cited: food inflation, geopolitical tensions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current economic conditions, considering both inflation and growth objectives.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation in India, particularly concerning supply-side shocks.
Examine the potential impact of this monetary policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy (e.g., banking, real estate, manufacturing).
Evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing price stability and economic growth in a volatile global environment.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo.
articles.trivia: The MPC consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Indian government.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Enhanced Climate Finance Commitments at G20 Finance Ministers Meet
articles.explainer:
• India advocated for stronger commitments on climate finance from developed nations at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting.
• Emphasized the need for easier access to green technologies and affordable finance for developing countries to meet their climate goals.
• Highlighted the importance of reforming Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) to better address climate change challenges.
• India presented its progress on domestic climate actions and renewable energy targets, urging a collaborative global approach.
• The meeting focused on accelerating energy transition, sustainable development, and mobilizing resources for climate action.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG)
prelimsPoints: G20 FMCBG meeting held on Jan 22, 2026.
India's key agenda: enhanced climate finance, MDB reform, green tech access.
Focus on accelerating energy transition and sustainable development.
India showcased domestic climate actions.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role and stance on climate finance within global forums like G20, linking it to the principle of CBDR.
Analyze the challenges developing countries face in accessing climate finance and green technologies.
Evaluate the necessity of MDB reforms to effectively address the scale of climate change financing needs.
Examine the geopolitical implications of climate finance negotiations and India's position as a bridge between developed and developing nations.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member countries.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court dismissed multiple petitions challenging the constitutionality of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The court affirmed that the Act strikes a reasonable balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• Key provisions related to cross-border data transfers and exemptions for government agencies were particularly scrutinized but upheld.
• The ruling provides clarity and strengthens the legal framework for data protection in India, crucial for the digital economy.
• Civil society groups expressed mixed reactions, with some welcoming the clarity while others raised concerns about potential government access to data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld DPDP Act, 2023 on Jan 22, 2026.
Act balances privacy rights with state interests (national security, public order).
Key provisions on cross-border data transfers and government exemptions upheld.
The ruling provides legal clarity for data protection in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the DPDP Act, particularly in light of the Right to Privacy judgment (Puttaswamy case).
Discuss the balance between individual privacy and state surveillance/national security concerns as addressed by the Act and the court.
Evaluate the impact of the DPDP Act on India's digital economy, foreign investments, and data localization debates.
Examine the challenges in implementing the DPDP Act, including capacity building for the Data Protection Board and awareness among citizens.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment of 2017.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

articles.headline: India's First Private Sector-Built Surveillance Satellite Successfully Launched by ISRO
articles.explainer:
• ISRO successfully launched a high-resolution earth observation satellite developed entirely by a private Indian aerospace firm, marking a significant milestone.
• The satellite, designed for advanced surveillance and imaging, will augment India's capabilities in national security, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This launch underscores the growing role of the private sector in India's space ecosystem, fostering innovation and reducing reliance on state-funded projects.
• The mission utilized ISRO's PSLV rocket, showcasing the synergy between public and private entities in space exploration.
• Experts believe this heralds a new era for India's space industry, promoting 'Make in India' and positioning the country as a global space hub.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: India's first private sector-built surveillance satellite launched on Jan 22, 2026.
Launched by ISRO using PSLV rocket.
Purpose: advanced surveillance, imaging, national security, disaster management.
Signifies increasing private sector role in Indian space industry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of private sector participation in India's space program and its implications for national development and global competitiveness.
Analyze how such collaborations between ISRO and private firms can accelerate technological advancements and indigenization in the space sector.
Evaluate the benefits of enhanced earth observation capabilities for various sectors, including defense, agriculture, and urban planning.
Examine the policy framework and regulatory challenges required to foster a thriving private space industry in India.
articles.imageDescription: ISRO PSLV rocket launching into space with a satellite.
articles.trivia: India's space economy is projected to reach $13 billion by 2025, driven by private sector growth.

articles.headline: National Wetland Conservation Plan Phase II Launched: Focus on Urban Wetlands and Blue-Green Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched the second phase of the National Wetland Conservation Plan (NWCP).
• This phase places a strong emphasis on protecting and restoring urban wetlands, recognizing their crucial role in climate resilience and biodiversity.
• Aims to integrate 'blue-green infrastructure' concepts into urban planning, using wetlands for flood mitigation, water purification, and recreational spaces.
• The plan includes community participation models, scientific management practices, and capacity building for local bodies.
• Seeks to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) related to water, sanitation, and sustainable cities through wetland preservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NWCP Phase II launched by Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Focus on urban wetlands and blue-green infrastructure.
Aims: flood mitigation, water purification, biodiversity conservation.
Includes community participation and scientific management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands, particularly urban wetlands, in the context of climate change and urbanization.
Analyze how the 'blue-green infrastructure' approach can contribute to sustainable urban development and climate resilience.
Evaluate the challenges in wetland conservation in India and how NWCP Phase II aims to address them.
Examine the role of community participation and inter-agency coordination in effective wetland management.
articles.imageDescription: A serene urban wetland with birds and greenery.
articles.trivia: Wetlands are considered Earth's most biologically diverse ecosystems, providing vital ecosystem services.

articles.headline: India-UK FTA Talks See Significant Progress on Key Sectors, Agreement Expected Soon
articles.explainer:
• Negotiators from India and the United Kingdom reported substantial headway in the ongoing Free Trade Agreement (FTA) discussions.
• Progress was noted across critical sectors including services, intellectual property rights, and specific industrial goods.
• Both sides expressed optimism for concluding the comprehensive deal in the near future, potentially boosting bilateral trade to $100 billion by 2030.
• Challenges remain on issues like visa liberalization for Indian professionals and tariff reductions on certain UK exports.
• The FTA aims to reduce trade barriers, enhance market access, and strengthen strategic partnership between the two nations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-UK FTA talks show significant progress.
Key sectors: services, IPR, industrial goods.
Target: bilateral trade to $100 billion by 2030.
Remaining challenges: visa liberalization, specific tariff reductions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-UK FTA for both countries in the post-Brexit global trade landscape.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for key Indian sectors (e.g., textiles, pharmaceuticals, IT services) from the proposed FTA.
Examine the role of visa liberalization and mobility of professionals as a crucial element in India's FTA negotiations with developed countries.
Evaluate how such bilateral agreements contribute to India's broader foreign policy and economic diplomacy objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom displayed together.
articles.trivia: The UK is one of India's oldest trading partners, with historical ties dating back centuries.

articles.headline: Indigenous AI Framework for Healthcare Launched: Enhancing Diagnostics and Personalized Treatment
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with leading research institutions, unveiled a national framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in healthcare.
• The framework aims to promote ethical deployment of AI tools for early disease detection, personalized treatment plans, and efficient healthcare delivery.
• Emphasizes data privacy, algorithmic transparency, and equitable access to AI-powered health solutions, especially in rural areas.
• Pilot projects are initiated in several public hospitals to test AI applications in radiology, pathology, and predictive analytics for disease outbreaks.
• This move aligns with India's broader 'Digital Health Mission' and 'Make in India' initiatives, fostering self-reliance in health technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Indigenous AI framework for healthcare launched by Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.
Aims: early disease detection, personalized treatment, efficient healthcare.
Focus on data privacy, ethical AI, equitable access.
Pilot projects in radiology, pathology, predictive analytics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence to revolutionize healthcare delivery in India, particularly in diagnostics and personalized medicine.
Analyze the ethical considerations and challenges associated with the deployment of AI in healthcare, including data privacy and algorithmic bias.
Evaluate how this indigenous AI framework aligns with India's broader public health goals and the 'Digital Health Mission'.
Examine the steps required to ensure equitable access to AI-powered health solutions across urban and rural populations.
articles.imageDescription: Doctors using AI tools to analyze medical scans.
articles.trivia: AI can analyze medical images 150 times faster than a radiologist, with 90% accuracy.

articles.headline: Border Infrastructure Development Accelerated in Eastern Sector to Counter External Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced an expedited plan for infrastructure development along India's eastern borders, particularly focusing on Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim.
• Projects include construction of new strategic roads, bridges, tunnels, and advanced surveillance systems to improve troop mobility and logistics.
• This acceleration is a direct response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and perceived external security challenges in the region.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing 'all-weather' connectivity to forward areas, crucial for rapid deployment and resource supply.
• The initiative aims to enhance India's defensive and offensive capabilities while integrating border communities into national development.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Linkages between development and spread of extremism.
prelimsPoints: Border infrastructure development accelerated in Eastern Sector (Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim).
Projects: strategic roads, bridges, tunnels, surveillance systems.
Aims to improve troop mobility and logistics.
Response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and external threats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure in India's eastern sector, considering geopolitical realities and national security imperatives.
Analyze the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude, rugged terrains and the innovative solutions being deployed.
Evaluate the impact of improved border connectivity on regional development, trade, and the integration of border communities.
Examine the role of organizations like BRO in enhancing India's border defense capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: Strategic road winding through mountainous terrain in the Himalayas.
articles.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Impact of Climate Change on Himalayan Agriculture, Calls for Adaptive Strategies
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) details the severe impact of climate change on agricultural practices in the Indian Himalayan Region (IHR).
• Findings indicate shifts in cropping patterns, increased pest infestations, and reduced yields due to erratic rainfall and rising temperatures.
• The report emphasizes the vulnerability of traditional farming communities and calls for urgent adoption of climate-resilient agricultural practices.
• Recommendations include promoting drought-resistant crops, precision farming, agroforestry, and strengthening early warning systems for farmers.
• Highlights the need for robust policy support and financial incentives for farmers to transition to sustainable and adaptive agricultural models.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, – different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: ICAR report on climate change impact on Himalayan agriculture released on Jan 22, 2026.
Key findings: shifts in cropping patterns, pest infestations, reduced yields.
Recommendations: drought-resistant crops, precision farming, agroforestry, early warning systems.
Highlights vulnerability of traditional farming communities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impacts of climate change on agriculture in the ecologically fragile Indian Himalayan Region.
Analyze the adaptive strategies and policy interventions required to build climate resilience among Himalayan farming communities.
Evaluate the role of scientific research and traditional knowledge in developing sustainable agricultural practices in mountainous regions.
Examine the implications of these agricultural changes for regional food security and socio-economic stability.
articles.imageDescription: Terraced farms in the Himalayas with changing weather patterns.
articles.trivia: The Indian Himalayan Region spans over 2,500 km and is a biodiversity hotspot, crucial for India's water security.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Export Promotion Scheme for MSMEs to Boost Global Competitiveness
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched a comprehensive export promotion scheme specifically designed to empower Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs).
• The scheme offers financial incentives, market access support, and capacity building programs to help MSMEs navigate international trade.
• Focuses on digitalization of export processes, easier access to export credit, and participation in international trade fairs.
• Aims to diversify India's export basket and increase the contribution of MSMEs to the national export target.
• This initiative is expected to create new employment opportunities and strengthen India's position in global supply chains.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: New export promotion scheme for MSMEs launched by Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
Offers: financial incentives, market access, capacity building.
Focus: digitalization, export credit, trade fair participation.
Aims to diversify exports and increase MSME contribution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of MSMEs in India's economic growth and employment generation, and the challenges they face in international trade.
Analyze how the new export promotion scheme addresses these challenges and enhances the global competitiveness of Indian MSMEs.
Evaluate the potential impact of this scheme on India's overall export performance and its integration into global supply chains.
Examine the importance of digitalization and access to finance for MSMEs to thrive in the international market.
articles.imageDescription: Small factory unit with 'Make in India' logo.
articles.trivia: MSMEs contribute approximately 30% to India's GDP and around 48% to the country's exports.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Progress Report 2025 Highlights Implementation Milestones
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 for the year 2025.
• The report highlights significant milestones achieved in foundational literacy and numeracy, vocational education integration, and teacher training.
• Noted progress in establishing multidisciplinary higher education institutions and promoting digital learning platforms.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access, especially in remote areas, and addressing teacher-student ratios.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to achieving the NEP's transformative goals by 2030, emphasizing continued reforms and stakeholder collaboration.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 Progress Report 2025 released by Ministry of Education.
Milestones: foundational literacy, vocational education, teacher training, multidisciplinary HEIs.
Challenges: equitable access, teacher-student ratios.
Goal: transformative goals by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key achievements of the National Education Policy 2020 in its initial years of implementation, as highlighted in the report.
Analyze the ongoing challenges in realizing the vision of NEP 2020, particularly concerning equitable access and quality education across diverse regions.
Evaluate the role of vocational education and multidisciplinary approaches in preparing India's youth for the future workforce.
Examine the importance of digital learning and teacher capacity building in transforming India's education landscape.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, possibly using digital devices.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the 1986 policy.

articles.headline: Coastal Security Strengthening Initiative Launched to Enhance Maritime Vigilance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs launched a multi-pronged initiative to bolster India's coastal security, focusing on enhanced surveillance and inter-agency coordination.
• The plan includes deploying advanced radar systems, improving patrol capabilities of the Coast Guard and Navy, and integrating fishing communities into the security apparatus.
• Emphasis on real-time information sharing between central and state agencies to counter maritime threats like illegal immigration, smuggling, and terrorism.
• Capacity building for coastal police forces and upgrading infrastructure at minor ports are also key components.
• This initiative aims to create a robust layered defense mechanism along India's vast coastline.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Internal security.
prelimsPoints: Coastal Security Strengthening Initiative launched by MHA.
Components: advanced radar, enhanced patrols (Coast Guard, Navy), fishing community integration.
Focus on real-time information sharing and inter-agency coordination.
Aims to counter illegal immigration, smuggling, terrorism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional threats faced by India's coastline and the importance of a comprehensive coastal security framework.
Analyze the role of various security agencies (Navy, Coast Guard, Coastal Police) and the need for seamless coordination in maritime security.
Evaluate the significance of integrating fishing communities into the coastal security apparatus as 'eyes and ears' on the sea.
Examine the technological advancements and infrastructure upgrades required to enhance India's maritime domain awareness.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard patrol boat on the sea.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of approximately 7,516 km, including island territories.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Unveil New Generation Quantum Computing Platform for Advanced Research
articles.explainer:
• A consortium of Indian research institutions and startups announced the development of a new generation quantum computing platform.
• The platform, based on superconducting qubits, aims to provide researchers with access to advanced quantum computational capabilities.
• This breakthrough is part of India's National Quantum Mission, positioning the country as a significant player in the global quantum race.
• Expected to accelerate research in materials science, drug discovery, and complex optimization problems.
• The initiative includes plans for training a skilled workforce in quantum technologies and fostering a vibrant quantum ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: New generation quantum computing platform unveiled by Indian scientists.
Based on superconducting qubits.
Part of India's National Quantum Mission.
Aims to accelerate research in materials science, drug discovery, optimization.
mainsPoints: Discuss the fundamental principles of quantum computing and its potential applications across various sectors, including defense, finance, and healthcare.
Analyze the significance of India's National Quantum Mission and the development of indigenous quantum computing platforms in the global technological landscape.
Evaluate the challenges in developing and scaling quantum technologies, including hardware limitations and the need for specialized talent.
Examine the ethical and security implications of quantum computing, particularly concerning cryptography.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum circuits and qubits.
articles.trivia: A classical computer bit can be 0 or 1, but a quantum bit (qubit) can be 0, 1, or both simultaneously (superposition).

articles.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission Progress Report: Over 70% Rural Households Now Have Tap Water Connections
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), indicating significant strides in providing tap water connections to rural households.
• The report states that over 70% of rural households across India now have functional household tap connections (FHTCs), up from 17% in 2019.
• Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, and West Bengal showed accelerated progress in the last year, while some northeastern states still face challenges.
• Focus areas for the upcoming year include ensuring water quality, sustainability of sources, and community participation in operation and maintenance.
• JJM aims to provide FHTCs to all rural households by 2024, contributing to public health and women's empowerment.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Jal Jeevan Mission progress report released on Jan 22, 2026.
Over 70% rural households now have FHTCs (up from 17% in 2019).
Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, West Bengal showed accelerated progress.
Future focus: water quality, source sustainability, community participation.
Mission target: FHTCs to all rural households by 2024.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of the Jal Jeevan Mission on public health, women's empowerment, and rural development in India.
Analyze the factors contributing to the success of JJM and the challenges encountered in its implementation across diverse states.
Evaluate the importance of community participation and local governance in ensuring the long-term sustainability of water supply schemes.
Examine the implications of universal tap water access for India's water security and broader Sustainable Development Goals.
articles.imageDescription: A woman filling water from a tap in a rural Indian village.
articles.trivia: The Jal Jeevan Mission was launched on August 15, 2019.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework to Promote Circular Economy Practices
articles.explainer:
• The NITI Aayog, in collaboration with relevant ministries, released a comprehensive policy framework to accelerate India's transition towards a circular economy.
• The framework identifies key sectors like electronics, plastics, construction, and automobiles for implementing circular economy principles (reduce, reuse, recycle).
• Aims to minimize waste generation, maximize resource efficiency, and create new economic opportunities in recycling and remanufacturing.
• Includes provisions for extended producer responsibility (EPR), green public procurement, and incentives for circular business models.
• This policy is crucial for India's sustainable development goals and reducing its environmental footprint.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: New policy framework for circular economy unveiled by NITI Aayog.
Key sectors: electronics, plastics, construction, automobiles.
Aims: minimize waste, maximize resource efficiency, new economic opportunities.
Provisions: EPR, green public procurement, incentives for circular models.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of a circular economy and its importance for India's sustainable development and resource security.
Analyze the key features of the new policy framework and its potential to transform waste management and resource utilization in identified sectors.
Evaluate the challenges in transitioning from a linear to a circular economy in India, including technological, financial, and behavioral aspects.
Examine the role of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and green public procurement in promoting circularity.
articles.imageDescription: Diagram showing the circular flow of resources in an economy.
articles.trivia: A circular economy aims to keep products and materials in use, regenerate natural systems, and design out waste and pollution.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Further Strengthening of Panchayati Raj Institutions for Local Governance
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted a report recommending significant measures to further empower Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in India.
• Recommendations include greater financial devolution, enhanced capacity building for elected representatives, and clarity on functional autonomy.
• Highlighted the need for better integration of PRIs in planning and implementing central and state welfare schemes.
• The report emphasized the role of PRIs in achieving grassroots democracy and sustainable rural development.
• Calls for regular elections, robust grievance redressal mechanisms, and increased participation of women and marginalized sections.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Panchayati Raj.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Panel recommends strengthening Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs).
Recommendations: financial devolution, capacity building, functional autonomy.
Emphasizes PRI role in scheme implementation, grassroots democracy.
Calls for regular elections, grievance redressal, women's participation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Panchayati Raj Institutions in strengthening grassroots democracy and promoting rural development in India.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Panel for empowering PRIs, focusing on financial autonomy and functional clarity.
Evaluate the challenges faced by PRIs in their effective functioning and the importance of addressing issues like capacity building and political interference.
Examine how PRIs can be better integrated into the planning and implementation of government welfare schemes to ensure last-mile delivery.
articles.imageDescription: A Gram Panchayat meeting in a rural Indian setting.
articles.trivia: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions.

articles.headline: India's First Indigenous Lithium-Ion Cell Manufacturing 'Gigafactory' Commences Operations
articles.explainer:
• India's first large-scale indigenous lithium-ion cell manufacturing facility, a 'Gigafactory', has officially commenced operations in Karnataka.
• This marks a critical step towards reducing India's dependence on imports for battery cells, crucial for electric vehicles (EVs) and renewable energy storage.
• The factory utilizes advanced technology for cell production, aiming to meet the rapidly growing domestic demand for high-capacity batteries.
• Supported by government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme, it is expected to boost local manufacturing and create numerous jobs.
• This development is vital for India's energy security, green mobility goals, and achieving net-zero emissions targets.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India's first indigenous Lithium-Ion Cell Manufacturing 'Gigafactory' commenced operations in Karnataka.
Aims to reduce import dependence for battery cells.
Crucial for Electric Vehicles (EVs) and renewable energy storage.
Supported by government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous lithium-ion cell manufacturing for India's energy security and green mobility ambitions.
Analyze the role of government policies like the PLI scheme in fostering domestic manufacturing and attracting investments in critical sectors like battery production.
Evaluate the potential impact of this Gigafactory on the growth of the electric vehicle industry and renewable energy adoption in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with establishing a complete battery manufacturing ecosystem, including raw material sourcing and recycling.
articles.imageDescription: Large-scale industrial facility for battery manufacturing.
articles.trivia: A 'Gigafactory' is a term coined by Tesla for its large-scale battery production facilities.

highlights: National Urban Transformation Mission (NUTM) Launched: A Blueprint for Sustainable Indian Cities with focus on smart infrastructure and green spaces.
10 Soldiers Killed, 10 Injured as Indian Army Casspir Falls into Gorge in Doda, J&K, prompting road safety and internal security concerns.
RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% in Q4 Monetary Policy Review, prioritizing inflation control amidst global uncertainties.
Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, providing clarity on privacy rights and state interests.
India's First Private Sector-Built Surveillance Satellite Successfully Launched by ISRO, marking a milestone in private space sector involvement.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Urban Transformation Mission (NUTM), consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims for holistic urban development across all urban local bodies in India.
2. It emphasizes public-private partnerships (PPPs) for effective implementation.
3. Climate-resilient infrastructure is a significant component of the mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The mission aims for holistic urban development across 100 cities, not all urban local bodies. Statement 2 is correct: The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships (PPPs). Statement 3 is correct: A significant allocation for climate-resilient infrastructure is a key focus.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for deciding the benchmark repo rate in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member committee, headed by the RBI Governor, responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023:
1. It provides for exemptions to government agencies from certain provisions for reasons of national security.
2. The Supreme Court has recently struck down key provisions related to cross-border data transfers.
3. It mandates a Data Protection Board of India to enforce its provisions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Act allows exemptions for government agencies on grounds of national security and public order. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Supreme Court upheld the constitutionality of the Act, including provisions related to cross-border data transfers and exemptions. Statement 3 is correct: The Act establishes a Data Protection Board of India to adjudicate non-compliance with its provisions.

prelimsMocks.question: India's first private sector-built surveillance satellite was recently launched by which of the following organizations?
prelimsMocks.explanation: ISRO successfully launched a high-resolution earth observation satellite developed entirely by a private Indian aerospace firm, utilizing its PSLV rocket.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary focus of Phase II of the National Wetland Conservation Plan (NWCP) recently launched?
options: (a) Protection of only Ramsar Sites in coastal areas.
(b) Emphasis on urban wetlands and integration of blue-green infrastructure.
(c) Exclusively funding international collaborations for wetland research.
(d) Focusing solely on large, rural wetlands for agricultural purposes.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Emphasis on urban wetlands and integration of blue-green infrastructure.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched NWCP Phase II, which places a strong emphasis on protecting and restoring urban wetlands and integrating 'blue-green infrastructure' concepts into urban planning.

mainsMocks.question: The launch of the National Urban Transformation Mission (NUTM) signifies India's commitment to sustainable urbanization. Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by Indian cities and how NUTM aims to address them, emphasizing the role of innovative governance and public-private partnerships. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NUTM's vision for sustainable urban development. Elaborate on key challenges like population growth, infrastructure deficit, pollution, housing, and mobility. Explain how NUTM's focus areas (smart infrastructure, green spaces, digital governance) and mechanisms (PPPs, citizen participation) address these. Conclude with the importance of integrated planning and execution for long-term impact.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court verdict upholding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, has significant implications for individual privacy and the digital economy. Critically analyze the balance struck by the Act between the right to privacy and legitimate state interests, and discuss its potential impact on data governance in India. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by contextualizing the DPDP Act and the SC verdict in light of the Puttaswamy judgment. Discuss the Act's key features, including definitions of data fiduciary/principal, consent, and data protection board. Analyze the 'balance' by discussing provisions for individual rights vs. exemptions for national security/public order. Critically evaluate concerns regarding state surveillance and discuss implications for digital economy, cross-border data flows, and individual trust.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for indigenous lithium-ion cell manufacturing through initiatives like the 'Gigafactory' is crucial for its energy security and climate goals. Discuss the strategic significance of this development for India's electric vehicle sector and renewable energy storage. What are the associated challenges in building a complete battery manufacturing ecosystem? (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Gigafactory and its importance. Explain how indigenous manufacturing reduces import dependence, boosts EV adoption, and supports renewable energy integration. Highlight its role in energy security and climate targets. Discuss challenges such as raw material sourcing (lithium, cobalt), technological expertise, recycling infrastructure, and competition with global players. Conclude with policy support needed.

quickBytes.text: The National Urban Transformation Mission (NUTM) targets 100 cities for holistic development.
The Indian Army's Casspir is an armored personnel carrier designed for protection against mines and ambushes.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members, three from RBI and three external.
The G20 represents approximately 85% of global GDP and over 75% of global trade.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, primarily used for launching earth observation and remote sensing satellites.
Wetlands are crucial for ecosystem services like flood control, water purification, and biodiversity support.
MSMEs contribute around 30% to India's GDP and nearly 48% to its exports.
Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide functional household tap connections to all rural households by 2024.
A circular economy focuses on reducing waste, reusing materials, and recycling products to minimize environmental impact.

GS2.headline: National Urban Transformation Mission (NUTM) Launched: A Blueprint for Sustainable Indian Cities
GS2.explainer:
• Prime Minister unveils the ambitious NUTM, aiming for holistic urban development across 100 cities over the next decade.
• Focus areas include smart infrastructure, sustainable mobility, green spaces, waste management, and digital governance.
• The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships (PPPs) and citizen participation for effective implementation.
• Aims to address challenges of rapid urbanization, improve liveability, and boost urban economic growth.
• Integrated with existing initiatives like Smart Cities Mission and Swachh Bharat Abhiyan, with a significant allocation for climate-resilient infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: NUTM launched by Prime Minister on Jan 22, 2026.
Targets 100 cities over 10 years.
Key pillars: infrastructure, mobility, green spaces, waste management, digital governance.
Emphasizes PPP model.
Integrates with Smart Cities Mission and Swachh Bharat Abhiyan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of NUTM in addressing India's urban challenges, linking it to SDGs.
Analyze the role of PPPs and citizen participation in achieving sustainable urban development.
Evaluate the potential impact of NUTM on economic growth, environmental sustainability, and social equity in urban areas.
Examine the challenges in implementing such a large-scale mission, including funding, coordination, and capacity building.
GS2.imageDescription: Conceptual image of a futuristic, green Indian city with smart infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030, making sustainable urban planning critical.

GS2.headline: India Pushes for Enhanced Climate Finance Commitments at G20 Finance Ministers Meet
GS2.explainer:
• India advocated for stronger commitments on climate finance from developed nations at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting.
• Emphasized the need for easier access to green technologies and affordable finance for developing countries to meet their climate goals.
• Highlighted the importance of reforming Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) to better address climate change challenges.
• India presented its progress on domestic climate actions and renewable energy targets, urging a collaborative global approach.
• The meeting focused on accelerating energy transition, sustainable development, and mobilizing resources for climate action.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG)
prelimsPoints: G20 FMCBG meeting held on Jan 22, 2026.
India's key agenda: enhanced climate finance, MDB reform, green tech access.
Focus on accelerating energy transition and sustainable development.
India showcased domestic climate actions.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role and stance on climate finance within global forums like G20, linking it to the principle of CBDR.
Analyze the challenges developing countries face in accessing climate finance and green technologies.
Evaluate the necessity of MDB reforms to effectively address the scale of climate change financing needs.
Examine the geopolitical implications of climate finance negotiations and India's position as a bridge between developed and developing nations.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member countries.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court dismissed multiple petitions challenging the constitutionality of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The court affirmed that the Act strikes a reasonable balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• Key provisions related to cross-border data transfers and exemptions for government agencies were particularly scrutinized but upheld.
• The ruling provides clarity and strengthens the legal framework for data protection in India, crucial for the digital economy.
• Civil society groups expressed mixed reactions, with some welcoming the clarity while others raised concerns about potential government access to data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld DPDP Act, 2023 on Jan 22, 2026.
Act balances privacy rights with state interests (national security, public order).
Key provisions on cross-border data transfers and government exemptions upheld.
The ruling provides legal clarity for data protection in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the DPDP Act, particularly in light of the Right to Privacy judgment (Puttaswamy case).
Discuss the balance between individual privacy and state surveillance/national security concerns as addressed by the Act and the court.
Evaluate the impact of the DPDP Act on India's digital economy, foreign investments, and data localization debates.
Examine the challenges in implementing the DPDP Act, including capacity building for the Data Protection Board and awareness among citizens.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment of 2017.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

GS2.headline: India-UK FTA Talks See Significant Progress on Key Sectors, Agreement Expected Soon
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiators from India and the United Kingdom reported substantial headway in the ongoing Free Trade Agreement (FTA) discussions.
• Progress was noted across critical sectors including services, intellectual property rights, and specific industrial goods.
• Both sides expressed optimism for concluding the comprehensive deal in the near future, potentially boosting bilateral trade to $100 billion by 2030.
• Challenges remain on issues like visa liberalization for Indian professionals and tariff reductions on certain UK exports.
• The FTA aims to reduce trade barriers, enhance market access, and strengthen strategic partnership between the two nations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-UK FTA talks show significant progress.
Key sectors: services, IPR, industrial goods.
Target: bilateral trade to $100 billion by 2030.
Remaining challenges: visa liberalization, specific tariff reductions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-UK FTA for both countries in the post-Brexit global trade landscape.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for key Indian sectors (e.g., textiles, pharmaceuticals, IT services) from the proposed FTA.
Examine the role of visa liberalization and mobility of professionals as a crucial element in India's FTA negotiations with developed countries.
Evaluate how such bilateral agreements contribute to India's broader foreign policy and economic diplomacy objectives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom displayed together.
GS2.trivia: The UK is one of India's oldest trading partners, with historical ties dating back centuries.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Progress Report 2025 Highlights Implementation Milestones
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 for the year 2025.
• The report highlights significant milestones achieved in foundational literacy and numeracy, vocational education integration, and teacher training.
• Noted progress in establishing multidisciplinary higher education institutions and promoting digital learning platforms.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access, especially in remote areas, and addressing teacher-student ratios.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to achieving the NEP's transformative goals by 2030, emphasizing continued reforms and stakeholder collaboration.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 Progress Report 2025 released by Ministry of Education.
Milestones: foundational literacy, vocational education, teacher training, multidisciplinary HEIs.
Challenges: equitable access, teacher-student ratios.
Goal: transformative goals by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key achievements of the National Education Policy 2020 in its initial years of implementation, as highlighted in the report.
Analyze the ongoing challenges in realizing the vision of NEP 2020, particularly concerning equitable access and quality education across diverse regions.
Evaluate the role of vocational education and multidisciplinary approaches in preparing India's youth for the future workforce.
Examine the importance of digital learning and teacher capacity building in transforming India's education landscape.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, possibly using digital devices.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the 1986 policy.

GS2.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission Progress Report: Over 70% Rural Households Now Have Tap Water Connections
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), indicating significant strides in providing tap water connections to rural households.
• The report states that over 70% of rural households across India now have functional household tap connections (FHTCs), up from 17% in 2019.
• Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, and West Bengal showed accelerated progress in the last year, while some northeastern states still face challenges.
• Focus areas for the upcoming year include ensuring water quality, sustainability of sources, and community participation in operation and maintenance.
• JJM aims to provide FHTCs to all rural households by 2024, contributing to public health and women's empowerment.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Jal Jeevan Mission progress report released on Jan 22, 2026.
Over 70% rural households now have FHTCs (up from 17% in 2019).
Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, West Bengal showed accelerated progress.
Future focus: water quality, source sustainability, community participation.
Mission target: FHTCs to all rural households by 2024.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of the Jal Jeevan Mission on public health, women's empowerment, and rural development in India.
Analyze the factors contributing to the success of JJM and the challenges encountered in its implementation across diverse states.
Evaluate the importance of community participation and local governance in ensuring the long-term sustainability of water supply schemes.
Examine the implications of universal tap water access for India's water security and broader Sustainable Development Goals.
GS2.imageDescription: A woman filling water from a tap in a rural Indian village.
GS2.trivia: The Jal Jeevan Mission was launched on August 15, 2019.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Further Strengthening of Panchayati Raj Institutions for Local Governance
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted a report recommending significant measures to further empower Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in India.
• Recommendations include greater financial devolution, enhanced capacity building for elected representatives, and clarity on functional autonomy.
• Highlighted the need for better integration of PRIs in planning and implementing central and state welfare schemes.
• The report emphasized the role of PRIs in achieving grassroots democracy and sustainable rural development.
• Calls for regular elections, robust grievance redressal mechanisms, and increased participation of women and marginalized sections.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Panchayati Raj.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Panel recommends strengthening Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs).
Recommendations: financial devolution, capacity building, functional autonomy.
Emphasizes PRI role in scheme implementation, grassroots democracy.
Calls for regular elections, grievance redressal, women's participation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Panchayati Raj Institutions in strengthening grassroots democracy and promoting rural development in India.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Panel for empowering PRIs, focusing on financial autonomy and functional clarity.
Evaluate the challenges faced by PRIs in their effective functioning and the importance of addressing issues like capacity building and political interference.
Examine how PRIs can be better integrated into the planning and implementation of government welfare schemes to ensure last-mile delivery.
GS2.imageDescription: A Gram Panchayat meeting in a rural Indian setting.
GS2.trivia: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions.

GS3.headline: 10 Soldiers Killed, 10 Injured as Indian Army Casspir Falls into Gorge in Doda, J&K
GS3.explainer:
• A tragic incident occurred in Doda district, Jammu and Kashmir, when an Indian Army Casspir vehicle veered off road and plunged into a deep gorge.
• The accident resulted in the immediate death of 10 soldiers and left 10 others seriously injured, raising concerns about road safety in challenging terrains.
• The Casspir, an armored personnel carrier, was reportedly part of a routine patrol in the mountainous region.
• Rescue operations were immediately launched by the Army and local authorities, with injured personnel airlifted to military hospitals.
• Investigations are underway to determine the exact cause of the accident, including potential mechanical failure or hazardous road conditions.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Internal security
prelimsPoints: Incident occurred in Doda, Jammu & Kashmir.
Vehicle involved: Indian Army Casspir.
Casualties: 10 killed, 10 injured.
Context: Routine patrol in mountainous terrain.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by Indian security forces operating in difficult terrains like J&K, including logistical and infrastructure issues.
Analyze the implications of such incidents on troop morale and operational effectiveness.
Suggest measures to enhance road safety and vehicle maintenance for military convoys in sensitive areas.
Examine the broader internal security challenges in Jammu and Kashmir and the role of the Indian Army.
GS3.imageDescription: Mountainous terrain of Jammu and Kashmir, with a military vehicle on a winding road.
GS3.trivia: The Casspir is a mine-protected ambush-protected (MRAP) vehicle originally developed in South Africa.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% in Q4 Monetary Policy Review; Focus on Inflation Control
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision aligns with market expectations, prioritizing inflation control amidst global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor highlighted persistent food inflation and geopolitical tensions as key risks to price stability.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Projections for Q4 FY26 GDP growth and inflation trajectory were also released, indicating a cautious optimism about economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI Repo Rate maintained at 6.5% on Jan 22, 2026.
Decision by Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Focus on inflation control, 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
Key risks cited: food inflation, geopolitical tensions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current economic conditions, considering both inflation and growth objectives.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation in India, particularly concerning supply-side shocks.
Examine the potential impact of this monetary policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy (e.g., banking, real estate, manufacturing).
Evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing price stability and economic growth in a volatile global environment.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo.
GS3.trivia: The MPC consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Indian government.

GS3.headline: India's First Private Sector-Built Surveillance Satellite Successfully Launched by ISRO
GS3.explainer:
• ISRO successfully launched a high-resolution earth observation satellite developed entirely by a private Indian aerospace firm, marking a significant milestone.
• The satellite, designed for advanced surveillance and imaging, will augment India's capabilities in national security, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This launch underscores the growing role of the private sector in India's space ecosystem, fostering innovation and reducing reliance on state-funded projects.
• The mission utilized ISRO's PSLV rocket, showcasing the synergy between public and private entities in space exploration.
• Experts believe this heralds a new era for India's space industry, promoting 'Make in India' and positioning the country as a global space hub.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: India's first private sector-built surveillance satellite launched on Jan 22, 2026.
Launched by ISRO using PSLV rocket.
Purpose: advanced surveillance, imaging, national security, disaster management.
Signifies increasing private sector role in Indian space industry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of private sector participation in India's space program and its implications for national development and global competitiveness.
Analyze how such collaborations between ISRO and private firms can accelerate technological advancements and indigenization in the space sector.
Evaluate the benefits of enhanced earth observation capabilities for various sectors, including defense, agriculture, and urban planning.
Examine the policy framework and regulatory challenges required to foster a thriving private space industry in India.
GS3.imageDescription: ISRO PSLV rocket launching into space with a satellite.
GS3.trivia: India's space economy is projected to reach $13 billion by 2025, driven by private sector growth.

GS3.headline: National Wetland Conservation Plan Phase II Launched: Focus on Urban Wetlands and Blue-Green Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched the second phase of the National Wetland Conservation Plan (NWCP).
• This phase places a strong emphasis on protecting and restoring urban wetlands, recognizing their crucial role in climate resilience and biodiversity.
• Aims to integrate 'blue-green infrastructure' concepts into urban planning, using wetlands for flood mitigation, water purification, and recreational spaces.
• The plan includes community participation models, scientific management practices, and capacity building for local bodies.
• Seeks to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) related to water, sanitation, and sustainable cities through wetland preservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NWCP Phase II launched by Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Focus on urban wetlands and blue-green infrastructure.
Aims: flood mitigation, water purification, biodiversity conservation.
Includes community participation and scientific management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands, particularly urban wetlands, in the context of climate change and urbanization.
Analyze how the 'blue-green infrastructure' approach can contribute to sustainable urban development and climate resilience.
Evaluate the challenges in wetland conservation in India and how NWCP Phase II aims to address them.
Examine the role of community participation and inter-agency coordination in effective wetland management.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene urban wetland with birds and greenery.
GS3.trivia: Wetlands are considered Earth's most biologically diverse ecosystems, providing vital ecosystem services.

GS3.headline: Indigenous AI Framework for Healthcare Launched: Enhancing Diagnostics and Personalized Treatment
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with leading research institutions, unveiled a national framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in healthcare.
• The framework aims to promote ethical deployment of AI tools for early disease detection, personalized treatment plans, and efficient healthcare delivery.
• Emphasizes data privacy, algorithmic transparency, and equitable access to AI-powered health solutions, especially in rural areas.
• Pilot projects are initiated in several public hospitals to test AI applications in radiology, pathology, and predictive analytics for disease outbreaks.
• This move aligns with India's broader 'Digital Health Mission' and 'Make in India' initiatives, fostering self-reliance in health technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Indigenous AI framework for healthcare launched by Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.
Aims: early disease detection, personalized treatment, efficient healthcare.
Focus on data privacy, ethical AI, equitable access.
Pilot projects in radiology, pathology, predictive analytics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence to revolutionize healthcare delivery in India, particularly in diagnostics and personalized medicine.
Analyze the ethical considerations and challenges associated with the deployment of AI in healthcare, including data privacy and algorithmic bias.
Evaluate how this indigenous AI framework aligns with India's broader public health goals and the 'Digital Health Mission'.
Examine the steps required to ensure equitable access to AI-powered health solutions across urban and rural populations.
GS3.imageDescription: Doctors using AI tools to analyze medical scans.
GS3.trivia: AI can analyze medical images 150 times faster than a radiologist, with 90% accuracy.

GS3.headline: Border Infrastructure Development Accelerated in Eastern Sector to Counter External Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced an expedited plan for infrastructure development along India's eastern borders, particularly focusing on Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim.
• Projects include construction of new strategic roads, bridges, tunnels, and advanced surveillance systems to improve troop mobility and logistics.
• This acceleration is a direct response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and perceived external security challenges in the region.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing 'all-weather' connectivity to forward areas, crucial for rapid deployment and resource supply.
• The initiative aims to enhance India's defensive and offensive capabilities while integrating border communities into national development.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Linkages between development and spread of extremism.
prelimsPoints: Border infrastructure development accelerated in Eastern Sector (Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim).
Projects: strategic roads, bridges, tunnels, surveillance systems.
Aims to improve troop mobility and logistics.
Response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and external threats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure in India's eastern sector, considering geopolitical realities and national security imperatives.
Analyze the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude, rugged terrains and the innovative solutions being deployed.
Evaluate the impact of improved border connectivity on regional development, trade, and the integration of border communities.
Examine the role of organizations like BRO in enhancing India's border defense capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: Strategic road winding through mountainous terrain in the Himalayas.
GS3.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

GS3.headline: Report Highlights Impact of Climate Change on Himalayan Agriculture, Calls for Adaptive Strategies
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) details the severe impact of climate change on agricultural practices in the Indian Himalayan Region (IHR).
• Findings indicate shifts in cropping patterns, increased pest infestations, and reduced yields due to erratic rainfall and rising temperatures.
• The report emphasizes the vulnerability of traditional farming communities and calls for urgent adoption of climate-resilient agricultural practices.
• Recommendations include promoting drought-resistant crops, precision farming, agroforestry, and strengthening early warning systems for farmers.
• Highlights the need for robust policy support and financial incentives for farmers to transition to sustainable and adaptive agricultural models.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, – different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: ICAR report on climate change impact on Himalayan agriculture released on Jan 22, 2026.
Key findings: shifts in cropping patterns, pest infestations, reduced yields.
Recommendations: drought-resistant crops, precision farming, agroforestry, early warning systems.
Highlights vulnerability of traditional farming communities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impacts of climate change on agriculture in the ecologically fragile Indian Himalayan Region.
Analyze the adaptive strategies and policy interventions required to build climate resilience among Himalayan farming communities.
Evaluate the role of scientific research and traditional knowledge in developing sustainable agricultural practices in mountainous regions.
Examine the implications of these agricultural changes for regional food security and socio-economic stability.
GS3.imageDescription: Terraced farms in the Himalayas with changing weather patterns.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Himalayan Region spans over 2,500 km and is a biodiversity hotspot, crucial for India's water security.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Export Promotion Scheme for MSMEs to Boost Global Competitiveness
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched a comprehensive export promotion scheme specifically designed to empower Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs).
• The scheme offers financial incentives, market access support, and capacity building programs to help MSMEs navigate international trade.
• Focuses on digitalization of export processes, easier access to export credit, and participation in international trade fairs.
• Aims to diversify India's export basket and increase the contribution of MSMEs to the national export target.
• This initiative is expected to create new employment opportunities and strengthen India's position in global supply chains.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: New export promotion scheme for MSMEs launched by Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
Offers: financial incentives, market access, capacity building.
Focus: digitalization, export credit, trade fair participation.
Aims to diversify exports and increase MSME contribution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of MSMEs in India's economic growth and employment generation, and the challenges they face in international trade.
Analyze how the new export promotion scheme addresses these challenges and enhances the global competitiveness of Indian MSMEs.
Evaluate the potential impact of this scheme on India's overall export performance and its integration into global supply chains.
